title: vitis rufotomentosa
intro: Vitis rufotomentosa, also known as the golden-leaved grapevine, is a species of grapevine native to the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective against various health issues. The health benefits of Vitis rufotomentosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support digestive health. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and essential oils for its medicinal benefits.
